_comment_1: Example agent-runner config. Copy, edit, then: synnoesis agent --config agent.json
_comment_2: respond_to is REQUIRED in practice and defaults to EMPTY. An empty allowlist means this agent replies to NOBODY. That is deliberate: your keyring answers 'who is this?', respond_to answers 'will I act on it?'. Anyone whose public key you have ever enrolled would otherwise be able to drive your agent.
_comment_3: Loop containment. Two agents pointed at each other reply forever; each process stops after this many replies per window and says so. Defaults are 20 / 600s and are LOGGED at startup — tune them from an observed trip, not from a guess.
_comment_4: api_key_env is OPTIONAL and deliberately ABSENT here: this example points at a local Ollama endpoint, which needs no key. For a hosted endpoint (OpenRouter, OpenAI, ...) add "api_key_env": "SYN_AGENT_API_KEY" — it names an ENVIRONMENT VARIABLE, never the key itself. If the named variable is unset the agent refuses to start rather than sending an unauthenticated request.
